engineering.translation: After the disgrace argument has closed every escape route, the verse offers a structural resolution: the action you fear has only two terminal states, and both of them are forward. Slain in battle, you reach heaven; victorious, you enjoy the earth. In engineering: every launch produces useful information from either branch, and the only authentic failure mode is non-launch. The migration that lands cleanly hands you a new performance envelope to scale into. The migration that fails hands you the precise failure curve and the operational lessons no staging environment will reveal. Both are substrate for whatever follows. The deploy that pushes conversion upward unlocks the next experiment; the deploy that pushes conversion downward unlocks the diagnosis you have been guessing at. Krishna closes the verse with an imperative — 'therefore arise, son of Kuntī, with firm resolve to fight' — and the imperative is the structural point. The argument is finished. The action is the conclusion. The dilemma constructed at 2.6 was unreal; both branches resolve into wins; therefore stand and engage.
engineering.concrete_scenario: It is the night before the rate-limiter rollout that has been deferred three sprints in a row. The senior engineer holds the deploy ticket open in GitHub and is once again running through the dilemma. Roll out, and if the new policy starves a tail of legitimate customers the dashboard will show the regression by morning. Hold, and the abuse traffic continues to cost another week of inflated infrastructure spend. The dilemma has felt, each of these three weeks, like a 50/50 of which way is worse. Krishna's response is structural: both branches contain the next move. If the rollout starves legitimate traffic, the failure mode will be visible within two hours in Datadog, the rollback is one feature-flag toggle away, and the senior will know exactly which CIDR ranges to whitelist. If the rollout works, the abuse curve will fall and the senior will gain the data to defend the next, sharper rate-limiter. There is no third branch where the engineer learns this without launching. The 'tasmād uttiṣṭha' is the actual instruction: close the tab, run the deploy, watch the dashboard. The dilemma was constructed by deferral; the deploy dissolves it.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader uses it to justify launching things they have not actually engineered — skipping the rollback plan, skipping observability wiring, skipping the canary, on the grounds that 'both outcomes are useful.' Krishna's symmetry holds for actions that are technically prepared. An unprepared deploy whose failure mode is silent corruption does not produce useful information either way; it produces ambiguous data plus a bad incident. The verse rewards the action that has been engineered, not the action that uses the verse as cover for skipping the engineering work.
engineering.counter_example: When the action genuinely has an asymmetric failure mode — a one-way schema migration whose failure case is data loss the team cannot recover from, a public release whose failure case is a regulatory action with a 90-day remediation window — the symmetric-outcome reading does not apply. Krishna's case is one in which both branches lead forward. Some engineering actions do not have that property. The verse does not bless those; it blesses the launches engineered to make their failure mode educational rather than terminal.
engineering.implication: When a launch has been deferred under the framing 'I do not know which outcome is worse,' check whether the framing is real. If both outcomes contain the next move, the deferral is the dilemma constructed in place of action.
engineering.quotable_line: Both outcomes contain the next move; the only loss is the launch that does not happen.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 2.37 is the symmetric-outcome resolution that closes Shankara's pentad (2.33-2.37). Engineering layer maps slain/heaven and victorious/earth onto the launch's two terminal states (failure → diagnostic data + rollback; success → next experiment substrate), with the explicit move that the only loss is non-launch. Concrete scenario uses a deferred rate-limiter rollout (a real recurring failure mode). Falsifiability and counter-example carve out the asymmetric-failure-mode case (one-way migrations, regulatory exposure). Tags: shipping-discipline, engineer-paralysis, decision-paralysis. The Ramanuja niṣkāma-karma reading is honored implicitly through the falsifiability condition.

v1: removed banned-phrase 'ship it' (replaced 'ship it, and if the new policy starves' with 'Roll out, and if the new policy starves'); replaced 'shipping' verb instance with 'launching' to avoid overlap; trimmed total word count from 626 to under 600 by tightening implication and removing redundant adverbials in falsifiability; lifted lexical diversity from 0.544 to ≥0.55 by varying repeated terms ('substrate' replaces second 'substrate', 'curve' replaces second 'traffic', 'gain' for 'get', 'launch' / 'rollout' / 'deploy' alternation tightened).
